title: aspidium auriculatum var. caespitosum
intro: Aspidium auriculatum var. caespitosum, also known as the hairy lip fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to parts of Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Aspidium auriculatum var. caespitosum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
